name: Bank Of Anguilla
description_short: Community bank serving the South Delta since 1904, offering checking, savings, loans, and deposit products with FDIC insurance and a focus on personal relationships.
description_long:
Bank of Anguilla is a traditional community bank that has operated continuously since 1904, serving two of the poorest counties in the nation and surrounding areas in Mississippi. The bank is deeply rooted in the South Delta region with locations in Anguilla, Cary, Mayersville, and Rolling Fork, operating on a mission of community service that has defined its operations for over 116 years.

The bank offers a full range of personal and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, personal loans, consumer loans, mortgage loans, agricultural loans, and small business loans. They provide modern banking convenience through online banking, mobile banking, mobile text banking, eStatements, and ATM access. The bank emphasizes financial education through their Financial Education Boot Camp program (currently suspended due to COVID) designed to help customers improve their financial literacy and money management skills.

Bank of Anguilla distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community banking and personal relationships rather than transactional banking. The bank employs local staff with deep connections to the area who understand the unique needs of individuals, families, and agricultural businesses. Their specialized lending products reflect this focus, particularly their agriculture loans targeting crop production, equipment purchases, and land improvements with lenders who "speak the language" of farming.

As a community bank, Bank of Anguilla faces inherent limitations in geographic reach and product sophistication compared to national banks. The bank's website shows signs of operational challenges, with the Financial Education Boot Camp still listing COVID-related suspension status. For consumers outside the four-location service area or those requiring sophisticated digital-only banking, this institution would not be suitable. The bank is best suited for individuals and businesses with geographic proximity to their locations who value relationship-based banking. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Consumer loans (auto, appliance, recreational vehicle, cash advances)
Agricultural loans (crop production, equipment, land improvements)
Small business loans and lines of credit
Mobile banking and mobile text banking
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
116+ years of continuous operation and community presence since 1904
Specialized agricultural lending with knowledgeable lenders familiar with farm financing
Multiple service channels including online banking, mobile banking, and mobile text banking
Personalized relationship-based banking model with locally-connected employees
Comprehensive loan products for personal, consumer, mortgage, business, and agricultural needs
Financial education programs available to help customers improve money management skills
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only four physical locations (Anguilla, Cary, Mayersville, Rolling Fork)
Financial Education Boot Camp program still suspended with no stated resumption date despite COVID ending
Website content appears outdated with references to temporary COVID closures and penny minting plans from 2025
No mention of competitive rates, APY information, or loan terms on website
Limited online presence with no clear information about digital banking capabilities or app features
best_for: Agricultural businesses and farmers needing specialized crop, equipment, and land improvement financing
Individuals and families in the South Delta region seeking relationship-based community banking
Small business owners in the four-location service area looking for personalized business lending
Consumers who prioritize personal relationships and local decision-making over digital convenience
diagnosis: Bank of Anguilla is best suited for individuals, families, and agricultural or small businesses located within or near their four Mississippi locations who value personalized, relationship-based banking over digital convenience and national reach. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this bank only serves a specific four-location area in the South Delta, making it unsuitable for consumers outside this region or those requiring the digital banking features of larger institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
